snippet.description:
We’re on the edge of a real shift: the “cloud” may stop being purely a terrestrial phenomenon and become a layered network that includes orbit. The strongest case isn’t that your favorite web app moves to space, but that space systems start acting like their own cloud region—compute, storage, and networking placed near satellites that generate massive amounts of data. If that happens, the first “clouds in space” won’t look like hyperscale campuses; they’ll look like compact, rugged orbital nodes that do AI inference, preprocessing, and caching, then beam results to Earth through high-throughput links. The big question is pace: in the near term, expect experiments and niche deployments for Earth observation, communications, and national security; mainstream adoption will require lower-cost launches, improved power and thermal designs, reliable optical crosslinks, and a clear cost advantage for specific workloads. Regulation and risk will shape it too—who owns the infrastructure, where the data “resides,” and how you secure something you can’t physically touch. So yes, we’re likely to see “clouds in space,” but as an extension of cloud architecture (edge + backbone), not a replacement for Earth regions—at least for a long while.

Bio

With over 30 years of experience in enterprise technology, David Linthicum is a globally recognized thought leader, innovator, and influencer in cloud computing, AI, and cybersecurity. He has authored over 17 best-selling books, over 7,000 articles, and 50 courses on LinkedIn Learning. He is also a frequent keynote speaker, podcast host, and media contributor on topics related to digital transformation, cloud architecture, AI, and cloud security.

snippet.description:
In this lecture, you will get a clear and easy introduction to Cloud Computing Delivery Models. The video explains the main features of cloud computing, such as virtualization, multi-tenancy, network access, scalability, and metering/chargeback, in a way that is simple for students and beginners to follow.
You will also learn about the three main cloud service models: SaaS, PaaS, and IaaS. The lecture compares these models to help you understand how they differ and where they are used in real life. In addition, the video discusses some important practical topics, including obstacles of cloud technology, cloud vulnerabilities, and common cloud challenges.
This lecture is useful for students preparing for exams, assignments, interviews, or building a basic understanding of how cloud computing works in the real world.
#CloudComputing #SaaS #PaaS #IaaS #Virtualization #CloudBasics #CloudSecurity #StudentLearning #ComputerScience #TechEducation

snippet.description:
The mainframe is not going anywhere. It is evolving. I just wrapped up a powerful conversation with Matt Whitbourne, VP of Product Management & Design for the BMC Software AMI portfolio.

We spoke about something many enterprises are quietly navigating right now:

How do you modernize the mainframe without breaking what already works?

Here’s what stood out to me from our discussion:

* Modernization is no longer optional. It is expected.
* Cyber resilience is now a board-level conversation.
* Data accessibility on the mainframe is becoming critical for AI and enterprise-wide analytics.
* Automation is helping teams move faster without compromising stability.

Matt also shared how AMI Cloud is evolving, especially around resilience, operational efficiency, and AI-driven capabilities.

What I appreciated most was this balance: 

Enterprises want innovation.
But they also want reliability.

The mainframe still runs some of the most critical systems in the world. The challenge is not replacing it. The challenge is transforming it. If you work in enterprise IT, data, or platform engineering, this conversation is worth your time.

In this episode of Visionary Voices, Paul Turner, Chief Product Officer for VCF at Broadcom, joins VMUG Executive Director Brad Tompkins to discuss the evolution of private cloud and what's ahead for modern application delivery.

From infrastructure as code and Kubernetes-native development to the convergence of IT ops and DevOps teams, Paul shares how VCF 9 is enabling organizations to deliver a true DevOps dial tone, giving developers the speed and agility of hyperscalers while keeping IT in control of governance, compliance, and cost. This conversation offers valuable insight for IT professionals navigating the shift back to private cloud and looking to maximize the full power of their VCF platform.

Everyone is talking about AI. But what actually powers it? At NVIDIA GTC, I spoke with Travis White from Zilliz at the Amazon Web Services (AWS) booth. Exciting conversation!!!!

We talked about vector databases. The layer that helps AI search through images, videos, and text, not just tables. Zilliz offers Milvus (open source) and a managed version on AWS. That means you can scale, stay secure, and avoid managing infrastructure. Real use cases are already here. Autonomous vehicles
Drug discovery, and a few more

You can also find Zilliz on AWS Marketplace and get started quickly. Simple idea. AI is not just models. It is the data layer underneath. Learn more from the conversation!!!!

snippet.description:
O Google mudou os limites do Antigravity essa semana silenciosamente e a galera tá furiosa. Usuários Pro pagando 20 dólares por mês travados por uma semana inteira. O que aconteceu: o refresh de 5 horas que todo mundo contava agora tem um cap semanal. Bateu o limite da semana, acabou. Espera dias pra resetar. E quando esgota o Google te oferece AI Credit Overages, basicamente pagar por token via Vertex API.


🔴 Formação Vibe Coding (Antigravity, Claude Code e +):  https://app.horadecodar.com.br/lp/formacao-vibe-coding?utm_source=yt_ag_limit_rate

🟪 Hospedagem para n8n que eu indico: https://hostinger.com.br/matheusbattisti (use o cupom HORADECODAR para ter +10% de desconto)

📘 Guia Engenharia de Prompt: https://app.horadecodar.com.br/ebookpages/guia-engenharia-de-prompt

Os planos ficaram assim. Free tem limite semanal, generoso pro começo mas acaba rápido em projetos sérios. Pro tem refresh de 5 horas mas com cap semanal, bateu o cap espera dias. Ultra tem refresh de 5 horas sem cap semanal, uso praticamente ilimitado. A galera no Reddit chamou de bait and switch. E tem gente reportando que o limit bateu sem ter usado quase nada, possivelmente um bug que tá sendo investigado.
 
Nesse vídeo dou 5 dicas práticas pra economizar tokens e trabalhar a semana inteira sem travar. A primeira dica sozinha corta 50 a 60% do consumo e é só uma mudança de hábito que leva 2 segundos. Tem a ver com o modo que você usa pra tarefas simples. A maioria tá usando o modo errado pra 80% do trabalho.
 
A segunda dica é sobre controlar o que o Antigravity lê do seu projeto. Toda vez que você manda um prompt o agente tenta entender o contexto e lê arquivos, analisa estrutura, indexa dependências. Se o projeto tem imagens, lock files e pastas de build ele tá gastando tokens com lixo. Mostro 3 arquivos que você configura uma vez e resolvem isso pra sempre. Existe até um repositório no GitHub com templates prontos pra Node, Python, Go e mais.
 
A terceira dica envolve mudar a forma como você dá feedback no código gerado. Tem um jeito que gasta uma fração dos tokens comparado com o que a maioria faz. Pode cortar 30 a 40% do consumo nas sessões de iteração que é onde a maioria estoura o limite.
 
As dicas 4 e 5 são sobre desligar o que não precisa e uma estratégia de seleção de modelos que a maioria não sabe que existe. Spoiler: as cotas são separadas por modelo e isso muda tudo.
 
O Antigravity continua sendo a melhor ferramenta grátis pra vibe coding em 2026. Mas agora você precisa ser estratégico. Quem usa sem pensar trava em 2 horas. Quem aplica essas 5 dicas trabalha a semana inteira sem problemas.


Entre no nosso servidor de Discord e me siga nas redes:

🟣 Discord Hora de Codar: https://discord.gg/Veq4mvsWwk
🔴 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/horadecodar/
🔷 Telegram: https://t.me/horadecodar

TIMESTAMPS

01:27 Porque o Google está limitando o Antigravity
03:17 Dica 1 para economizar tokens no Antigravity
05:00 Dica 2 para bater o limite do Antigravity
06:14 Dica 3 para não travar no  Antigravity
07:45 Dica 4 para usar mais o Antigravity antes de limitar
09:36 Dica 5 reduz o uso de tokens no Antigravity
11:40 Minhas considerações sobre o limite de tokens do Antigravity

snippet.description:
👍Try AGBCLOUD Today: https://bit.ly/4r3RTly

AGBCLOUD is a cloud sandbox platform that gives AI agents a secure place to actually run work — not just describe it. Instead of relying on “chat-only” AI that can’t safely access tools, files, or compute, AGBCLOUD provides isolated sessions powered by reusable environments (images) where agents can execute real code, process real data, and produce real outputs. In this video, we build an Automated Data Analyst using the official AGBCLOUD SDK, following the Quick Start guide step by step. We create a clean Code Space session, generate a realistic 200-row sales dataset, clean and validate it, then compute key business insights such as total revenue, top products, channel performance, weekly revenue trends, discount impact, and anomaly detection. Next, we automatically generate visual charts and an executive-ready Markdown report — all inside the sandbox. Finally, we download the results (CSV, charts, and report) to a local outputs folder so they’re ready to share with a team or drop into a presentation. The goal is to show how AGBCLOUD bridges the gap between conversational AI and real task automation, making it practical to build reliable, repeatable workflows with isolation, control, and simple copy-paste setup using the SDK.

https://github.com/agbcloud

#AGBCLOUD #AIautomation #AIAgents #DataAnalytics #AutomatedReporting #PythonDeveloper #SDKIntegration #CloudComputing #BusinessIntelligence #NoCodeAI

BridgeMind just exploded past $3k MRR and we’re not slowing down. Today on Day 149, we’re vibe coding live with MRR at $3,446 and ARR at $41,352, pushing the BridgeMind stack even closer to escape velocity. This stream is all about shipping massive product updates while the graph keeps bending up and to the right.

💰 The Journey to $1,000,000
Current MRR: $3,446
Current ARR: $41,352

🚀 GET BRIDGESPACE: https://bridgemind.ai
💬 Join 7,000+ Builders: https://bridgemind.ai/discord

We’re rolling out big upgrades to BridgeSpace and BridgeVoice that make agentic coding even more hands‑free and production‑ready. Inside BridgeSpace, we’ll be orchestrating agent swarms across Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro, routing work through Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini to show what a real multi-model production workflow looks like. The new BridgeVoice loop lets you speak your intent once and have it automatically engineered and dispatched to the best model for the task.

We’ll be dogfooding everything live—fixing bugs, shipping features, and showing exactly how I use this stack every day to build BridgeMind in public. What We’re Doing Live Today

Shipping new BridgeSpace features for smoother agent swarms and multi-model workflows across Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT 5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini

Upgrading BridgeVoice for faster, more accurate voice-to-code, with better routing into Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini-based agents

Using the full BridgeMind stack live to build, debug, and ship production features

Talking through growth: crossing $3k MRR, aiming for $5k by end of month and $10k MRR by May

What is BridgeMind?
BridgeMind is the operating system for the agentic future. We’re building a fully integrated suite of vibe coding tools designed to orchestrate complex AI workflows:

BridgeMCP — agentic memory and context routing across models like Claude Opus 4.6, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro

BridgeSpace — AI-native Agent Development Environment (ADE) for managing swarms powered by Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini

BridgeVoice — high-velocity voice-to-text for coding at the speed of thought, optimized for multi-model routing

BridgeCode — the agentic engine powering autonomous coding workflows from natural language to production code

snippet.description:
No API keys. No cloud. No per-token cost. Just your Mac.

In this video, I show you how to run Arcee AI's Trinity Mini (26B parameters, 3B active) locally on Apple Silicon using MLX, and wire it up to OpenCode as a fully local AI coding assistant. Everything runs on-device — ideal for air-gapped environments, regulated industries, or anyone who wants a private coding AI.

I cover the full setup: choosing the right quantization for your hardware, benchmarking generation speed across all quantizations, and configuring OpenCode to talk to the local model. I also check Trinity's results with Claude Code!

  ⭐️⭐️⭐️ More content on Substack at https://www.airealist.ai ⭐️⭐️⭐️

  *** MODELS
  Arcee Trinity Mini (base) → https://huggingface.co/arcee-ai/Trinity-Mini
  Trinity Mini MLX 8-bit → https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Trinity-Mini-8bit
  Trinity Mini MLX 6-bit → https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Trinity-Mini-6bit
  Trinity Mini MLX 4-bit → https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/Trinity-Mini-4bit
  Trinity Mini on OpenRouter (free) → https://openrouter.ai/arcee-ai/trinity-mini:free

  *** CODE
Full walkthrough + scripts → https://github.com/juliensimon/arcee-demos/tree/main/trinity-mini-mlx

  *** TOOLS
  MLX by Apple → https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx
  mlx-lm (model serving) → https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-lm
  OpenCode (terminal coding assistant) → https://opencode.ai
  Arcee AI → https://www.arcee.ai

snippet.description:
In this video, I show you how to install Azure Local in a Hyper‑V virtual machine, making it easy to evaluate Azure Local in your HomeLab or at work—without requiring dedicated physical hardware.
I’m Thomas Maurer, EMEA Global Black Belt (GBB) for Sovereign Cloud at Microsoft, and in this walkthrough you’ll see how Azure Local can be deployed in a nested virtualization setup for learning, testing, and proof‑of‑concept scenarios.
Whether you’re an IT pro, cloud architect, consultant, or platform engineer, this video helps you understand:

- How Azure Local works in practice
- What’s possible in an evaluation or demo environment
- How to get hands-on experience before moving to production

# 🔍 What you’ll learn in this video

- Running Azure Local inside a Hyper‑V VM
- Using Azure Local for HomeLab and work evaluations
- Key considerations and limitations of virtualized setups
- When to use this approach vs. physical hardware

# ⚠️ Important note
This setup is intended for evaluation, learning, and demo purposes only. Production deployments of Azure Local require certified physical hardware.

# 📌 Why Azure Local?
Azure Local brings Azure services closer to where your data lives, enabling hybrid and sovereign cloud scenarios with consistent operations, security, and management—especially important for regulated industries and sovereign environments.
If you’re exploring hybrid cloud, sovereign cloud, or edge scenarios, this is a great place to start.

👍 If you find this useful, don’t forget to like, subscribe, and share—and drop your questions or HomeLab ideas in the comments!

# 📖 Chapters
00:00 Intro
5:37 Prepare Active Directory
9:49 Download Azure Local Software
11:35 Create VM and Install OS
22:24 Set up Azure Environment
27:17 Register Azure Local Node
31:50 Deploy Azure Local

# ⭐ About
Thomas is a EMEA Global Black Belt (GBB) for Sovereign Cloud at Microsoft, helping organizations across the region harness Microsoft’s sovereign, hybrid, multicloud, and edge solutions. He works directly with C-level leaders and technical teams to remove roadblocks, accelerate digital transformation, and shape long-term cloud strategies that meet business and regulatory needs.
With over 15 years of experience in cloud and datacenter, including his previous role as Principal Program Manager and Chief Evangelist for Azure Hybrid, he combines deep technical expertise with a passion for customer success. He specializes in creating scalable architectures, solution accelerators, and thought leadership that drive adoption and innovation on Azure.
Thomas is a passionate community advocate and frequently speaks at industry events such as Microsoft Ignite and Microsoft TechDays. 
If you want to know more about Thomas, check out:

🌍  Website / blog - https://www.thomasmaurer.ch
🐦 X.com - https://www.twitter.com/thomasmaurer
💼 LinkedIn - https://www.linkedin.com/in/thomasmaurer2/

#Microsoft #AzureLocal #SovereignCloud
